title: sorbus aucuparia subsp. amurensis
intro: Sorbus aucuparia subsp. amurensis, also known as the Amur rowan, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthocyanins, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as immune system support and antimicrobial properties. Sorbus aucuparia subsp. amurensis is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
